# D0501 Diploma of Higher Education Arts - Media communication stream

D0501 Handbook entry: refer to the Handbook for authorised course and unit information. Please note that the links in this map will direct you to the current version of the handbook entry. You can access previous versions of course and unit information by using the ‘year switcher’ located within a current course or unit entry.

# Pathways to Monash degrees

If you pass all units in the Diploma of Higher Education – Arts Media Communication stream on the first (and only) attempt and achieve a minimum weighted average mark of $60 %$ in the diploma, you will be eligible to enrol in a Bachelor of Media Communication\* and will receive credit for up to 48 points for units passed in the diploma.

\*This destination course is offered at the Caulfield campus

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Stream | Campus | Mode | Duration |
| Media communication | Clayton/Caulfield | On-campus | 1 year |
| Sem | Core units |  | Media Communication Units |
| 1 | EDF1010Learning in auniversity context(6 pts) | ATS1206Media challenges(6 pts) | BMC specialisation 1 unit -level 1(6 pts) | BMC specialisation 2 unit -level 1(6 pts) |
| 2 | EDF1011Knowledge and contextOREDF1012Mathematics for tertiarystudy(6 pts) | ATS1119Communicating in thedigital era(6 pts) | BMC specialisation 1 unit -level 1(6 pts) | BMC specialisation 2 unit -level 1(6 pts) |

# Notes:

● Credit of up to 48 credit points.

● Students will be required to attend Caulfield campus for a minimum of 2 units.

In addition to EDF1010 and EDF1011, you must complete six level 1 units as follows: a. Two level 1 core units: • ATS1206 Media challenges • ATS1119 Communicating in the digital era b. Four units from any two of the following specialisations. You must take both units which appear under the specialisation: Journalism • ATS1089 Fundamentals of journalism • ATS1090 Practice of journalism Media • ATS1279 Media and culture • ATS1280 Understanding the media Screen • ATS1304 Introduction to television studies ATS1305 Introduction to film studies Public relations • ATS1125 Public relations foundations ATS1060 Celebrities, influencers and promotion
